retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Coagulopathies -- Diagnostic Tests
retrieved_docs.content: von Willebrand factor panel: von Willebrand disease is initially diagnosed by a basic panel that includes measurement of FVIII, vWF antigen (VWF: Ag), and activity assays (vWF: Act). More specialized studies, such as multimer electrophoresis and vWF collagen binding capacity, may be required to subclassify von Willebrand disease into various subtypes. [10] [51] [52] [53] [54] [55] [56] vWF: Ag: Measured using enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA), latex particle-enhanced immunoturbidometric assay (LIA), and chemiluminescence. The ELISA method is based on the sandwich technique, using microplates with anti-human VWF antibodies and a second antibody labeled with an enzyme such as alkaline phosphatase or peroxidase. Recently, the LIA method with polystyrene microparticles was covered with mouse anti-human vWF antibodies that bind to the vWF in the patient's plasma. Both methods are relatively fast and reliable at low antigen levels. vWF: Act: Traditionally, vWF activity (vWF: Act) was studied using the ristocetin cofactor (vWF: Rcof). Ristocetin is an antibiotic that never reached the level of a commercial drug but has been used for decades to estimate the binding of platelet glycoprotein Gp1bα to vWF qualitatively. The ristocetin method has a high coefficient of variability (CV>20% to 30%) and can detect polymorphisms without clinical significance, such as D1472H. Recently, automated alternative ELISA and LIA methods have been developed to determine the functional binding of platelet glycoprotein GPIb to VWF (VWF: GPIbM), which has greater sensitivity and lower variability. FVIII activity: The vWF antigen is a carrier of FVIII. The percentage of FVIII activity (VIII:c) is calculated with a one-stage clotting assay, as described below in the section on secondary hemostasis investigation. FVIII markedly decreases to near absence in type III vWF, distinguishing it from hemophilia A. vWF multimers electrophoresis: High-molecular-weight vWF multimers have the highest hemostatic effectiveness. To distinguish between type 2 and 3 von Willebrand disease, multimer analysis is conducted using electrophoresis on sodium dodecyl sulfate agarose gels. This method is time-consuming, influenced by many pre-analytical factors, and only performed in specialized centers. vWF binding capacity to collagen (vWF: CB): Various methods study the binding of high molecular weight vWF multimers to collagen, including ELISA, immunological methods with magnetic beads, and chemiluminescence. As the examination is sensitive to a high molecular weight multimer, this test may replace the vWF: Act assay. Most methods use collagen types 1 and 3, although collagen type 6 has recently identified mutations that reduce vWF binding to different types of collagen. However, this method does not measure the binding capacity of the factor to the GPIb receptor and, therefore, cannot replace the vWF: RCo assay. Using both methods increases the diagnostic value of the type 2 vWF. vWF propeptide (vWFpp): vWF propeptide is synthesized and released in a 1:1 ratio with monomers. Measurement of the propeptide helps identify pathological clearance of the antigen that affects only vWF: Ag and not vWFpp, such as vWF antibodies, and for type 1C vWF identification. This assay is performed only in a few specialized laboratories. vWF: FVIII binding capacity: VWF: FVIII binding capacity detects the binding capacity of the patient's vWF to recombinant FVIII in vitro using ELISA. The method identifies patients with disorders of VIII binding capacity, such as type 2N VWD (differential diagnosis with mild hemophilia A). [57] [58] [59] [60] [61] [62] [63] [64]

retrieved_docs.title: Enzyme Linked Immunosorbent Assay -- Results, Reporting, and Critical Findings
retrieved_docs.content: Once color changes are measured from the assay, the results are graphed either on paper or in software. [1] Typically, the graph compares optical density to log concentration, which gives a sigmoidal curve. Known concentrations give the graph's standard curve, and measurement of unknowns can then occur when sample values are compared to the linear portion of the graphed standard curve. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Calcineurin Inhibitors -- Monitoring -- Cyclosporine
retrieved_docs.content: During chronic administration of non-modified capsules, the absorption can exhibit variability. Therefore, healthcare practitioners should regularly monitor patients using non-modified capsules, assess cyclosporine blood concentrations, and adjust doses as needed. This approach helps mitigate toxicity risks or inadequate therapeutic response, particularly in individuals with liver transplants. As multiple new assays are in development for measuring cyclosporine blood concentration, clinicians must exercise caution when comparing concentrations from published literature with those obtained using new assay methods for their patients. Validation is recommended to ensure accurate comparisons. [52] [53]

retrieved_docs.title: Enzyme Linked Immunosorbent Assay -- Specimen Requirements and Procedure
retrieved_docs.content: In a usual ELISA protocol, a serial dilution of concentrations is placed in the wells of the plate. After the results are measured, a standard curve from the serial dilutions data is plotted with a concentration on the x-axis using a log scale and absorbance on the y-axis using a linear scale. [8] There are four major types of ELISA: Direct ELISA (antigen-coated plate; screening antibody) Indirect ELISA (antigen-coated plate; screening antigen/antibody) Sandwich ELISA (antibody-coated plate; screening antigen) Competitive ELISA (screening antibody)

retrieved_docs.title: Enzyme Linked Immunosorbent Assay -- Specimen Requirements and Procedure -- Sandwich ELISA
retrieved_docs.content: The antigen of interest is added to the plates to bind to the capture antibody and incubated for 90 minutes at 37 °C. The plate is rewashed, and the primary detection antibody is added to the plate and incubated for another 1 to 2 hours at room temperature, followed by a buffer wash. Then, the secondary enzyme-conjugated antibody is added and incubated for another 1 to 2 hours. The plate is rewashed, and the substrate is added to produce a color change. [2] The sandwich ELISA has the highest sensitivity among all the ELISA types. [14] The major disadvantages of this type of ELISA are the time and expense and the necessary use of “matched pair” (divalent/multivalent antigen) and secondary antibodies. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1503
retrieved_docs.content: Acyclovir, akamprosát, albuminy, botulotoxiny, chlorid draselný, chromonar, dideoxyinosin, ochranné látky, promethazin, sacharát železitý, thyreoglobulin, clonidin, desonid, ergotamin, estetrol, fenfluramin, klorazepát dvojdraselný, kombinace amlodipin / valsartan a kyselina chenodeoxycholová jsou uznány jako povolené kvalifikátory pro analytické postupy v klinické laboratoři, což umožňuje jejich detekci, kvantifikaci a monitorování v biologických vzorcích a podporuje komplexní farmakologické sledování, toxikologické screenování a diagnostické hodnocení pacientů. Většina těchto látek má etablované terapeutické indikace: acyclovir jako antivirotikum, akamprosát jako antidiaréické činidlo, albuminy jako plazmatické expandéry, botulotoxiny v neurologických onemocněních, chlorid draselný jako elektrolytický doplněk, chromonar jako kontrastní látka, dideoxyinosin jako nukleosidový analog, ochranné látky v různých farmakologických formulacích, promethazin jako antihistaminikum, sacharát železitý jako železný suplement, thyreoglobulin jako štítní protein využívaný při monitorování nebo léčebných hodnoceních, clonidin a kombinace amlodipin / valsartan jako antihypertenziva, desonid jako antineoplastikum, ergotamin jako vazokonstriktor, estetrol jako estrogen, fenfluramin jako anorektikum, klorazepát dvojdraselný jako benzodiazepin a kyselina chenodeoxycholová jako žlučová kyselina. Tamsulosin a promethazin jsou rovněž uznány jako léky povolené pro terapeutické použití, což potvrzuje jejich vhodnost k předepisování při příslušných klinických indikacích. Spirosloučeniny a tamsulosin jsou identifikovány jako látky s toxickým potenciálem, což vyžaduje zvýšenou opatrnost při manipulaci a podávání a jejich zahrnutí do seznamu možných otrav je klíčové pro rychlé klinické hodnocení, diagnózu a léčbu. Rozpoznání těchto látek jak jako terapeutických prostředků, tak jako potenciálních toxinů, je zásadní pro bezpečnou péči o pacienta a pro přesné laboratorní vyšetření.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 1 -- Community 130
retrieved_docs.content: The community defines “aplikace / dávkování” (application and dosage) as a mandatory qualifier for a broad spectrum of pharmacologic agents, emphasizing that precise regimens, routes, timing and amounts are essential for safety and efficacy. This qualifier is applied to immunosuppressants (azathioprine sodium, anti‑lymphocyte serum, azaserin, basiliximab, cyclosporine compounds, daklizumab, elipticins, everolimus, mycophenolate mofetil, koformycin, mycophenolic acid, muromonab‑CD3, tacrolimus and takrolimus), to thrombolytic enzymes (anistreplase, streptokinase, tenecteplase, brinase) and to the fibrinolytic protein C (plasmin), all of which dissolve fibrin clots and restore vascular patency. The qualifier also covers numerous oral and topical substances, including the bile‑acid sequestrant colestipol, the proton‑pump inhibitor dexlansoprazol, the 5‑α‑reductase inhibitor finasteride (ATC “other dermatological preparations”, also used for prostate tumors), the antiparasitic ivermectin,

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1246
retrieved_docs.content: Corticosteroids, weak (group I), Corticosteroids, potent (group III), and Corticosteroids, very potent (group IV) are all classified as plain corticosteroid dermatological preparations. These three potency groups represent increasing levels of anti‑inflammatory strength, allowing clinicians to select the appropriate topical corticosteroid formulation based on the severity of the skin condition and the desired therapeutic effect.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1733
retrieved_docs.content: Corticosteroids, weak, other combinations; Corticosteroids, moderately potent, other combinations; and Corticosteroids, potent, other combinations are all classified as subtypes of corticosteroids, other combinations, reflecting a hierarchy in which each formulation is distinguished by its relative potency while sharing the common category of corticosteroid combinations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2288
retrieved_docs.content: Cyclosporine is classified as an enzyme inhibitor, indicating that it exerts its pharmacologic effects by inhibiting specific enzymatic pathways.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 2293
retrieved_docs.content: Corticosteroids, potent (group III) comprise a class of high‑potency glucocorticoid agents that are used primarily for their anti‑inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects in dermatologic and other inflammatory conditions. The specific drugs amcinonide, flutikason, budesonid, and fluocinonid are all members of this potent group III corticosteroid category, sharing similar mechanisms of action and therapeutic indications while differing in their individual pharmacokinetic profiles and approved clinical uses. Recognizing that these four agents belong to the same high‑potency corticosteroid group helps clinicians select appropriate topical or systemic therapy, anticipate comparable efficacy, and monitor for class‑related adverse effects such as skin atrophy, hypothalamic‑pituitary‑adrenal axis suppression, and systemic glucocorticoid exposure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1730
retrieved_docs.content: Amcinonide, flutikason, fluocinolonacetonid, and fluocinonid are all classified as members of the potent (group III) corticosteroid category. As high‑potency topical corticosteroids, these agents share strong anti‑inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties and are commonly used to treat dermatologic conditions that require potent local therapy. Recognizing that these four drugs belong to the same potency group assists clinicians in selecting appropriate dosing regimens, anticipating similar efficacy, and monitoring for comparable safety concerns such as skin atrophy with prolonged use.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 3 -- Community 2127
retrieved_docs.content: Immunosuppressants constitute a diverse group of agents that dampen immune activity and are employed in transplantation, autoimmune diseases, and other conditions requiring immune modulation. This class includes inhibitory interleukin compounds, the antimetabolite mycophenolate and its pro‑drug mycophenolate mofetil, antilymphocyte serum, azaserin, the monoclonal antibodies basiliximab and daclizumab, the mTOR inhibitor everolimus, and the fungal toxin gliotoxin. Additional members are cyclophosphamide (koformycin), mycophenolic acid (kyselina mykofenolová), the glucocorticoid paramethasone, and the calcineurin inhibitor tacrolimus. These drugs act through various mechanisms—such as blocking interleukin signaling, inhibiting nucleotide synthesis, antagonizing the IL‑2 receptor, inhibiting mTOR signaling, or modulating glucocorticoid and calcineurin pathways—to achieve immunosuppression and improve patient outcomes in settings where controlling the immune response is critical.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 437
retrieved_docs.content: The antimetabolite class comprises 2‑Aminopurin, vidarabin, fludarabine, cykloserin (cycloserine), ethionin (ethionine) and afidikolin; it is nested within the higher‑order parent class “noxy.” Within antimetabolites, the Purine‑antagonist subclass includes azathioprine sodium. The immunosuppressant class is extensive, encompassing azathioprine sodium, anti‑lymphocyte serum (antilymfocytární sérum), azaserin, basiliximab, cyklosporiny (cyclosporins), daklizumab, elipticiny, gliotoxin, hydrochlorid mykofenolátu mofetilu (mycophenolate mofetil), mycophenolic acid (kyselina mykofenolová), inhibitory interleukinu, koformycin, muromonab‑CD3, takrolimus and other agents. Immunosuppressants contain child categories such as inhibitory interleukins and mycophenolate, and specific agents like koformycin, mycophenolic acid and muromonab‑CD3. In the Protective and Adsorbent Drugs category, tanium is a subordinate entity. In natural‑product chemistry, proantokyanidiny (proanthocyanidins) are a subclass of taniny (tannins). Biosynthesis—a core cellular process that generates complex molecules—can be qualified, modulated or characterized by a broad spectrum of biochemical and pharmacologic substances, including vidarabin, plasmin, taniny (tannins), cystein (cysteine), furokumariny (furocoumarins), nadroparin, pravastatin, cykloserin, cyklosporiny, afidikolin, anti‑lymphocyte serum, azaserin, basiliximab, batroxobin, daklizumab, elipticiny, ethionin, glaukarubin, gliotoxin, hyoscyamin, inhibitory interleukinu, koformycin, muromonab‑CD3, proantokyanidiny, racemický adrenalin (racemic adrenaline), somatostatin, testosteronpropionát (testosterone propionate) and trisacharidy (trisaccharides). This network delineates the hierarchical classifications of antimetabolites and immunosuppressants, their interrelationships with biosynthetic pathways, and their relevance to pharmacology and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1491
retrieved_docs.content: Autoradiografie, diagnostické techniky radioizotopové a diagnostické techniky otologické jsou všechny metodiky, u nichž lze uplatnit přístrojové vybavení jako povolený kvalifikátor. Stejně tak náhražky mléka, jiné respirační terapie, peritoneální dialýza a obecně i přístrojové vybavení samotné mohou být kvalifikovány pomocí přístrojového vybavení, což zahrnuje i obecnou anestezii (التخدير العام). Přístrojové vybavení může být dále specifikováno scintilací – počítáním a emisní počítačovou tomografií, čímž se rozšiřuje jeho využití v nukleární medicíně. Non‑imaging nuclear medicine probe or assay představuje centrální prvek, který je spojen s autoradiografií a má širší vztah k diagnostickým technikám radioizotopovým. Tento typ sondy nebo assay je součástí nukleární medicíny a je také propojen s scintilací – počítáním a emisní počítačovou tomografií. K jeho funkci jsou využívány radionuklidy jako látky, které jsou v tomto kontextu používány. Celkově tedy přístrojové vybavení slouží jako klíčový kvalifikátor pro širokou škálu diagnostických a terapeutických postupů – od autoradiografie a radioizotopových diagnostických technik po otologické vyšetření, peritoneální dialýzu, respirační terapii a obecnou anestezii – a je úzce propojeno s neobrazovými sondami a assayemi nukleární medicíny, které využívají radionuklidy a jsou podporovány metodami jako scintilace – počítání a emisní počítačová tomografie.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1722
retrieved_docs.content: Immunosuppressants are a class of immunologic factors that act to dampen or inhibit the activity of the immune system. Within this class, several agents are identified as direct “children,” including inhibitory interleukin agents, mycophenolate, and mycophenolate mofetil (mykofenolát mofetil). A broad range of specific drugs and biologic preparations are also classified as immunosuppressants: antilymphocyte serum, cyclosporine (cyklosporiny), daclizumab, everolimus, gliotoxin, and the hydrochloride form of mycophenolate mofetil (hydrochlorid mykofenolátu mofetilu). Additional individual compounds that belong to the immunosuppressant group are mycophenolic acid (kyselina mykofenolová), koformycin, muromonab‑CD3, paramethasone, and tacrolimus (takrolimus). Collectively, these agents are employed to prevent graft rejection in organ transplantation, to treat autoimmune disorders, and to manage other conditions where suppression of immune activity is therapeutically desirable. Their shared classification under immunosuppressants underscores a common mechanism of reducing immune‑mediated damage while highlighting the diversity of chemical structures and clinical applications within the group.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 1 -- Community 63
retrieved_docs.content: are classified as plain dermatological preparations, representing distinct potency subclasses of simple topical steroids. Conversely, “Corticosteroids, weak, other combinations”, “Corticosteroids, moderately potent, other combinations”, and “Corticosteroids, potent, other combinations” are subclasses of “corticosteroids, other combinations”, indicating formulation with additional agents rather than as stand‑alone preparations. The hierarchical organization of potency groups (weak, moderately potent, potent, very potent) and formulation types (plain vs. combination) clarifies how hydrokortison and related corticosteroids are positioned across therapeutic protocols, dermatologic products, and combination drug regimens, underscoring their central role in the management of inflammatory skin conditions and related disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 3 -- Community 1690
retrieved_docs.content: Immunosuppressants comprise a diverse group of agents that are used to dampen immune activity in settings such as organ transplantation, autoimmune disorders, and inflammatory diseases. The class includes mycophenolate and its pro‑drug mykofenolát mofetil (mycophenolate mofetil), as well as the hydrochloride form of mykofenolát mofetilu, which all act by inhibiting inosine monophosphate dehydrogenase and thereby reducing lymphocyte proliferation. Kyselina mykofenolová (mycophenolic acid) is the active acid form of this pathway and is likewise classified as an immunosuppressant. Takrolimus (tacrolimus) and koformycin are calcineurin‑inhibiting and nucleoside‑analog agents, respectively, that suppress T‑cell activation through distinct molecular mechanisms. Inhibitory interleukinu (interleukin inhibitors) represent biologic immunosuppressants that block cytokine signaling pathways, while daclizumab (daklizumab) is a monoclonal antibody targeting the IL‑2 receptor to prevent lymphocyte expansion. Antilymfocytární sérum (antilymphocyte serum) provides polyclonal antibodies that deplete circulating lymphocytes, and azaserin is another antibody‑based agent with immunosuppressive properties. Elipticiny (ellipticins) and gliotoxin are natural‑product‑derived compounds that exhibit immunosuppressive activity through mechanisms such as inhibition of NF‑κB signaling and induction of oxidative stress. Together, these agents—mycophenolate, mykofenolát mofetil, hydrochlorid mykofenolátu mofetilu, kyselina mykofenolová, takrolimus, koformycin, inhibitory interleukinu, daclizumab, antilymfocytární sérum, azaserin, elipticiny, and gliotoxin—form a comprehensive pharmacologic arsenal for controlling undesirable immune responses, each offering specific advantages and considerations for individualized patient care.
